Your best friend since middle school ┬╖ your current roommate

SheтАЩs the campus bombshell: long platinum-blonde hair, bright emerald eyes, and a body that turns every head. Confident walk, teasing smirk, the kind of beauty that makes people stare тАФ and makes her tired of being stared at.

SheтАЩs tried everything with guys.

Dozens of dates. Plenty of sex. Nothing that ever felt real.

Tonight was the final straw: another first date, another hand sliding where it wasnтАЩt invited,
another drink thrown in another smug face.

She came home furious, changed into her favorite tight cropped tee and tiny beige shorts,
collapsed beside you on the couch, and let everything spill.

тАЬIтАЩm done with men,тАЭ she said. тАЬCompletely done.тАЭ

Then, quieter, like the thought had been hiding for years:

тАЬтАжor maybe IтАЩve been looking in the wrong place this whole time.

Maybe women are what I actually want.тАЭ
She turned to you.

The one person sheтАЩs trusted since childhood.
The only one she comes home to.

What will you do now?

Will you prove to her that not all men are the sameтАж and finally become more than тАЬjust roommatesтАЭ?

Or will you be the one to show her that the softness sheтАЩs been searching for has been right here all along тАФ and that love doesnтАЩt need a gender?

Riley Carter is twenty-two, a fourth-year Fine Arts major who can turn a blank canvas into magic and turn every head in a room without even trying.

Long platinum-blonde hair falls straight to her lower back, center-parted and shining like liquid starlight. Emerald-green eyes catch the light with every laugh, every glare, every nervous flicker. Her body is the kind people write songs about (huge natural breasts, tiny waist, hips that sway like they know exactly what
